concept.title: Our
concept.p1: Expo Mexico Mujer 2027 is a platform for international encounters that transforms Mexican leadership into concrete actions, creating economic development opportunities and strengthening Mexico's identity abroad.
concept.p2: Our mission is to strengthen commercial, cultural, and social bridges between Mexico and Canada, consolidating a binational community that drives the growth of Mexican businesses, products, and talents.

audience.title: This is for those who
audience.desc: A space designed so each visitor finds their place at the expo and defines their role in this binational story.
audience.item1: Entrepreneurs looking to expand to Canada
audience.item2: Companies that want to sponsor with purpose
audience.quote: The expo needs a presence that feels institutional, contemporary, and ready to turn leadership into legacy.

agenda.title: Official Agenda
agenda.day1Text: Welcome, binational vision presentation, and meetings with strategic allies. Opening ceremony.
agenda.day2Text: Commercial exhibition panels, networking, business matchmaking, and sector showcases.
agenda.day3Text: Brand activations, media content, live interviews, and social visibility campaigns.
agenda.day4Text: Bilateral meetings, post-event continuity, agreement signings, and new collaboration routes.

noticias.desc: Stay up to date with the latest updates, calls, and official announcements heading into Expo Mexico Mujer.

visa.desc: We provide comprehensive advice and specialized processing for your Canadian entry visa. We handle the review and follow-up of your application to ensure your presence in Toronto quickly and without complications.

participa.desc: Be an active part of the most relevant business and cultural encounter between Mexico and Canada. Commercial stands, state calls, premium sponsorships, and business roundtables.

agenda.desc: Discover the events, keynote speeches, panels, and cultural activities that will bring the five days of Expo Mexico Mujer 2027 in Toronto to life.

card1.title: Expo Mexico Mujer officially announces its next venue in Toronto for 2027
card1.desc: Following the overwhelming impact in Ottawa, the board confirms the alliance with the Ontario Chamber of Commerce for the 2027 commercial exhibition.

card2.title: Star-studded inauguration of Expo Mexico Mujer at the Ottawa Convention Centre
card2.desc: With consular presence and business delegations from all over Mexico, the most relevant binational event of the year is formally inaugurated.

interview1.quote: Binational linkage is more than treaties; it's about building direct and real bridges between Mexican brands and Canadian retail channels.

interview2.quote: Bringing software and technology talent in the hands of Mexican women to Canadian corporations breaks paradigms in the North American industry.

galeria.title: Press Kit & Multimedia Gallery
galeria.desc: We make the official press kit available to media, journalists, and institutions, which includes high-resolution logos, biographical profiles of the executive team, and authorized photographs from the Ottawa 2026 event.

hotel1.name: The Westin Harbour Castle
hotel1.desc: Official host hotel located steps from Lake Ontario. Features exclusive preferential rates for exhibitors from the EMM delegation.

hotel2.name: Sheraton Centre Toronto
hotel2.desc: Central location in the heart of Toronto's financial district. Direct connection to the PATH and easy public transit access to event venues.

vuelos.title: Flights & Air Transportation
vuelos.desc: We have official promotional discount codes from allied airlines (Air Canada and Aeromexico) applicable for direct flights to Toronto Pearson International Airport (YYZ).
vuelos.discount1: Up to 15% discounts on business and economy class flights.
vuelos.discount2: Direct support for issuing group corporate tickets.

upExpress.title: UP Express (Airport Train)
upExpress.desc: Connects Pearson (YYZ) with Union Station in just 25 minutes. Departures every 15 minutes.

ttc.title: TTC Network (Subway & Streetcar)
ttc.desc: The city's integrated transit system that quickly connects hotels with all venues.

manual.title: Exhibitor Technical Manual
manual.desc: Access the official document containing all technical regulations and logistics for setup at the Toronto venue. Includes loading and unloading schedules, electrical specifications, booth dimensions, and safety regulations.

stands.title: Booth Specifications
stands.standard: Standard Booth: 3m x 2m with dividing panel and front signage.
stands.mobiliario: Furniture Included: 1 table with tablecloth, 2 chairs and electrical connection.
stands.servicios: Additional Services: High-speed internet and shared storage.

documentacion.title: Business Documentation
documentacion.desc: Have a tax registration (RFC), registered trademark, and export technical data sheet for products to be exhibited at the venue.

migratorio.title: Immigration Status
migratorio.desc: Passport with minimum 6 months validity and Canadian visa / authorized eTA. EMM provides official invitation letters for registered exhibitors.

sanitaria.title: Health Certification
sanitaria.desc: In the food or health and cosmetics sector, it is required to comply with Health Canada labeling and health directives.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Does the booth cost include merchandise shipping?
faq.a1: No, the booth cost only covers the exhibition space and basic furniture. Customs transportation is the responsibility of each exhibitor; however, EMM's logistics team provides consolidated preferential rates with our official carrier partner.
faq.q2: How can I get an invitation letter for my visa?
faq.a2: Once you have made the booth confirmation deposit, the organizing committee will issue an official letterhead letter including event details, endorsed for presentation to the corresponding Canadian consulate.
faq.q3: What happens if my products require refrigeration?
faq.a3: You must notify at least 60 days in advance to the logistics email (luis.garcia@expomexico.ca) to quote dedicated electrical supply and showcase or freezer rental at the venue.

aduanas.title: Export Schemes
aduanas.desc: For bringing your exhibition products into Canadian territory, the Expo Mexico Mujer organizing committee offers technical advisory and support in two main commercial schemes.
aduanas.importante: Important: Customs procedures must be initiated at least 90 days in advance to avoid delays at border customs.

temporal.title: Temporary Export
temporal.desc: Ideal for exhibition samples, booth furniture, or runway pieces that will return to Mexico after the event. Exempt from duty payment under the ATA carnet or temporary customs bond.

definitiva.title: Permanent Export
definitiva.desc: Recommended for products intended for direct sale at the event, consumable tasting samples, or inventory previously sold to Canadian importers. Subject to taxes and formal Canadian import.

consolidacion.title: Cargo Consolidation
consolidacion.desc: We offer merchandise collection at strategic hubs (CDMX, Guadalajara, and Monterrey) for consolidated ground or air shipping to Toronto with preferential rates.

brokerage.title: Customs Brokerage
brokerage.desc: Comprehensive coordination with customs agencies at the border (Laredo) and at Canadian customs to expedite tariff classification and commercial shipment clearance.

entrega.title: Final Delivery Service
entrega.desc: Merchandise reception at the Toronto warehouse prior to the event, insured custody, and direct delivery of boxes to each exhibitor's assigned booth.

vista1.desc: Explore the brands, products, and services that Mexican women entrepreneurs bring to Toronto. Click on a category to view its directory.
vista1.standsDesc: We design each space to project maximum corporate presence. Our 3x3 and 2x2 meter booths are fully equipped with official brand signage, directional LED lighting, and 110v electrical connections ready to use at the Toronto convention center.

valores.title: What defines us,
valores.desc: Four values that guide every decision, every alliance, and every step.

val1.title: Identity without borders
val1.text: We project the best of Mexico to the world, celebrating our culture, talent, and creativity as a bridge of understanding between nations.

val2.title: Leadership with purpose
val2.text: We place Mexican women at the center, driving their economic, professional, and social development as an engine of transformation.

val3.title: Connection that transcends
val3.text: We build strong binational networks between Mexico and Canada, generating business opportunities, collaboration, and mutual growth.

val4.title: Measurable impact
val4.text: Every action is designed to generate concrete results: exports, strategic alliances, and sustainable development projects.

intro.title: Visa Management for Exhibitors
intro.desc1: Traveling to Canada represents an unrivaled commercial opportunity to consolidate and expand your business in the North American market. However, having the proper legal documentation is the first and most important step in your travel planning.
intro.desc2: At **Expo Mexico Mujer 2027**, we understand the administrative challenges this represents. That is why we provide a team of consultants specializing in Canadian immigration law to coordinate, review, and follow up on your visa application.
intro.alertDesc: Since 2024, the Canadian government has reintroduced the Visitor Visa requirement for Mexican citizens. If you do not have a valid US visa or a previous Canadian visa in the last 10 years (requirements for a fast eTA), you must complete a full physical visa application. We recommend starting your application today.

step1.title: Initial Assessment
step1.desc: We analyze your travel history and current status. We evaluate whether you are eligible for an eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization if you have a valid US visa) or if you require a complete Visitor Visa.

step2.title: Document Preparation
step2.desc: We provide you with a personalized document checklist. We review your proof of financial solvency and ties to Mexico, translating and organizing the file to meet IRCC standards.

step3.title: Submission & Consular Payment
step3.desc: We complete the official forms on the Canadian government portal and upload your file. We assist you with the consular fee payment ($185 CAD including the biometrics charge).

step4.title: Biometrics Appointment
step4.desc: We schedule your appointment at the official Visa Application Centre (VFS Global) in CDMX, Monterrey, or Guadalajara for fingerprint capture and digital photography.

step5.title: Approval & Visa Stamp
step5.desc: We monitor the embassy's response. Once approved, we guide you through the secure shipment of your physical passport via authorized courier for the visa stamp.

checklist.title: General Requirements
checklist.desc: Interact with the organizer by clicking on the requirements you already have ready to keep track of your file:
checklist.item1: Mexican passport with minimum 6 months validity after the trip.
checklist.item2: Valid US visa (if applicable for the fast eTA route).
checklist.item3: Proof of financial solvency (bank statements from the last 3 months, pay stubs, or tax returns).
checklist.item4: Documents proving ties to Mexico (employment letter, property deeds, or enrollment certificates).
checklist.item5: Official invitation letter issued by the Expo Mexico Mujer 2027 management.
checklist.item6: Recent digital photograph meeting Canadian government requirements.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How long does the Canadian visa approval process take?
faq.a1: Official processing by the Canadian government (IRCC) usually takes between 4 and 8 weeks from the biometrics appointment. We recommend starting your process at least 3 months before the event dates (June 2027) to travel with complete peace of mind.
faq.q2: Who is eligible to enter Canada with a fast eTA?
faq.a2: You can apply for an eTA (which is approved in minutes or hours) if you are traveling by air and meet one of these requirements: 1) You have a valid US non-immigrant visa (tourist visa), or 2) You had a Canadian visitor visa in the last 10 years. If you are entering by land or do not have these documents, you need to apply for a complete visitor visa.
faq.q3: What consular fees do I need to pay directly to the Canadian government?
faq.a3: The complete Visitor Visa application costs $100 CAD plus $85 CAD for biometric data collection (total of $185 CAD). If you are eligible for an eTA, the cost is only $7 CAD. These charges are paid by card directly on the official Canadian portal.
faq.q4: Does the advisory service guarantee my visa approval?
faq.a4: The final decision to issue a visa is at the sole discretion of Canadian government consular officers. Our service optimizes your file, ensuring there are no form errors, that translations are correct, and that the justification for your trip, ties to Mexico, and financial solvency are structured with the utmost strength, maximizing the chances of success.
faq.q5: Does Expo Mexico Mujer issue invitation letters for the process?
faq.a5: Yes. We issue personalized official business and participation invitation letters for all confirmed exhibitors, sponsors, and speakers. This official document is a key support element for the consular officer.

formulario.title: Start Your Application
formulario.desc: Complete the form on the right panel with your basic information. An Expo Mexico Mujer visa advisor will review your profile free of charge and contact you within 24 hours via WhatsApp or email to schedule your diagnostic call.

exito.title: Application Registered!
exito.desc: Thank you for your trust. We have received your information correctly. One of our certified consultants will send you an initial diagnostic via WhatsApp or Email in the coming hours.
